entry.title: Re: SAP Event Mesh: Event Connector to Microsoft Azure to go Beta
entry.content:
Hi @KStrothmann,

what is the approach are you refering to? Which will allow us to connect to the Azure Event Grid and push/get events between Event Mesh and Event Grid?

I got through entitlements and I can see event-hub (but it seems to be only SAP to SAP). Can you please elaborate more on how to connect with azure event grid?

Thanks

Vlado

entry.title: Re: How to handle errors for semantic key with number ran...
entry.content: Your scenario is best handled by deferring the number range assignment until after validations have passed. Instead of using a determination on save, use a late determination after successful validation to generate the semantic key. This ensures that numbers are only assigned when the transaction is committed, preventing gaps. Additionally, store errors in a buffer during validation and block the save if needed. This approach avoids losing number range values due to failed transactions.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...
entry.content: Yes, Ariba integration for a Multi-ERP (Federated Process Control - FPC) architecture can support S/4HANA Public Cloud as one of the backends alongside other SAP backends in a Parent-Child Ariba Realm setup. However, technical feasibility depends on Ariba Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG) configurations, master data harmonization, and transaction orchestration across multiple ERPs. Ensure that Ariba Network and Procurement Operations can route transactions appropriately based on ERP assignment. Consulting SAP Ariba documentation and SAP support is recommended for detailed setup guidelines.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Copy from Upload Version to Public Version: Overwrite issue
entry.content:
Hi , 

Thanks for your reply, I forgot to mention in advanced formula I had this configuration "CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF".

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
@mark_foerster, your point is to what I understand, you trained with a table without ID column, you predict with a table without ID column?

If so

  • easiest would be to add_id to the fit dataframe
  • or drop the ID column from the predict dataframe, the latter I assume is what you would like to avoid.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...

But I don't want to add a description on how to get a .pfx file - I don't want to get in to the discussion of certificate file formats and I also want to keep the explanation with separate public and private key files.

entry.title: Re: C4C how to default corporate email template in service ticket

Hi,

Any updates on this topic? I have a customer requesting the same functionality.

Has SAP enabled this functionality yet?

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Evolution : Upcoming New Major Updates in 1H 2025 Releases
entry.content:
The Evolution of Recruitment: Why It Matters

Before diving into the upcoming features, it’s important to understand the broader trend in recruitment. As talent acquisition becomes more data-driven and candidate-centric, companies are looking for ways to simplify workflows while simultaneously enhancing the hiring experience. SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting has been continuously innovating to meet these needs, and 2025 will be no different.

The new features coming in  1H 2025 are designed to address both current industry demands and the future of recruiting — where Artificial Intelligence (AI), automation, data analytics, and mobile-first tools are key. With these updates, SAP SuccessFactors aims to help businesses improve their recruitment strategies and stay ahead in an increasingly competitive talent market.

1H 2025 Release: Innovations to Improve Recruitment Efficiency
The first half of 2025 will bring several key features focused on automation, candidate experience, and integrating AI to streamline recruitment. These innovations are designed to help recruitment teams reduce manual tasks, speed up hiring processes, and ultimately make better hiring decisions.

1. Career site Similar Jobs Component
As part of 1H 2025 release it will provide a specific component to add in career site builder which will help candidates to see similar jobs that may also be a good fit.
It will Identify similar jobs based on an automated matching algorithm and show to candidates

2. Resume upload Hero Component
One of the most exciting features in the 1H 2025 release to provide a customizable Hero Component for the AI skills resume upload match, in which IT will leverage AI and machine learning to better match the posted  jobs based on their skills, experience etc. mentioned in the resume and defined in Job Description of the posted Jobs.

3. New Reimagined Interface for Candidate Profile
In the 1H 2025 release, SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting will introduce new refreshed personalized candidate page. Which will help candidate to access their information in a neatly organized view, easy and intuitive to navigate relevant information and it will also ensure brand consistency with improved styling and theming.

4. New Candidate Job Apply Experience
As part of 1H 2025 release to provide, it will improve the the experiences Job Apply process to
better streamline the collection of applicant data, Here is some key benefits of this new apply process

  • Streamline the application process with a guided wizard tailored to meet your unique hiring needs
  • Dynamically capture applicant responses using branching questions
  • Ensure brand consistency with improved styling and theming

5. New Improvement in New Application Workbench
As part of 1H 2025 release, we will get a collection minor improvements to the new applicant workbench like 

  • Recruiting Team will able to contact agency instead of the applicant
  • Drag & Drop applicants into different statuses like old application dashboard
  • Print or save applications 
  • Access referral and agency information on the application detail page

6. AI - Assisted Interviews and Interview Feedback Summarization 
As part of 1H 2025 release is the enhancement of AI-powered, Generative AI Summaries of interviews and interview, here is below benefits of this feature

  • Review interviewer feedback with an AI consolidated summary
  • Gain additional perspectives on completed interviews with AI insights derived from the transcripts.
  • Manually adjust summaries as needed to ensure ethical evaluation of all interviewees

 

Preparing for the 1H 2025 SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Updates

As we approach to 1H 2025, consultants and businesses should begin preparing for these exciting new features. Here are a few key recommendations:

  • Train your recruitment team: Ensure your recruitment team is up-to-date with the new features and understands how to make the most of AI-powered tools, mobile capabilities, and predictive analytics.
  • Review and refine your recruitment strategy: Align your recruitment strategy with the new features to enhance your workflows, streamline communication, and improve candidate experience.
  • Leverage SAP SuccessFactors’ robust reporting: Use the advanced analytics capabilities to continuously assess and optimize your recruitment processes for better outcomes.

 

Note: The above blog is based on latest announcements & upcoming features. For the latest updates, please refer to SAP’s official communications.

Looking forward to catching up with you soon with a new blog!

entry.title: Re: How to represent manual email one swimlane to another...
entry.content: As far as I know, message symbols do not have to be modelled within a pool, as the process flow already describes an information interaction. We use it for external interaction. Additionally, I accept the warning as it is only a warning. (You can also switch off the warning message in the modeling conventions.)

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
Hello @jeetendrakapase 

wow, you really put a lot of effort into these Q&A! Very helpful. 😊

One question regarding No. 12, embedded SAC: I'm not sure where I read or heard it, but I think I understood that the embedded SAC in S/4 HANA Public Cloud would disappear sooner or later?

And my most anticipated question about the Foundation services, is unfortunately still not answered satisfactory 🤔 : Wow exactly will SAP handle the Data Delta Extraction technically, from S4 CDS Views into the Data Lake Files?

Will there be lots of Notes or a huge TCI be required to be implemented in S4 or will it be the same technology like today when e.g. using Replication Flows into SAP Datasphere? It would be really helpful if we could see a picture/slide about those technical details soon 🤗

Thank you very much.

Martin

entry.content: Hi, yes the tabs and dates you mention here is aligned with the previous explanation.

entry.title: Re: How to run an alvGrid in mb51 using BDC?
entry.content:
Try to set opt-nobinpt to abap_true, you should also

  • Go to SPRO-Materials Management-Inventory and Physical Inventory Inventory-Reporting - Set up print functions for reporting and then read IM activity documentation (especially the GRID/LIST checkbox).
  • But as @Sandra_Rossi wrote don't expect to control the ALV Grid execution.

entry.title: Re: How to handle errors for semantic key with number ran...

entry.title: The key field of the dropdown values comes from the API.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

On the visit object, I have added certain values to a standard field. I am sending a request using the standard API, the visitVisitTypeCollection API. While the description field appears normally in the development system, it appears as a code in the production system. How can I fix this?

 

Note: The field is not a custom field but the standard 'Visit Type' field.

 

 

 

Saygılarımla,

Kadir

entry.title: Removing "View Checklist Result" from Learning History in SAP SF LMS
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I’m looking for guidance on a UI customization within the SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System. Specifically, is there any way to remove or hide the “View Checklist Result” from the learning history displayed in the user interface?

Background:

Scenario: Our end users are seeing the “View Checklist Result” in their learning history, and we’d prefer to streamline the interface by removing this element.

Attempts: I’ve reviewed the configuration settings and UI options available in the LMS administration, but haven’t found a direct way to disable or remove this view.

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
Hello Andreas,

thanks a lot for your reply. I had already asked Claude, and got some very similar information. This is my base table for input:

This is the offending code:

j

 

I am really at a loss here. Just 4 data columns, all are of type DOUBLE. And the table starts with an integer column ID. And still HANA_ML complains about column 0 having the wrong SQL type. I am at a loss here what is causing this:

So I read the documentation and still it won't work. Is there a way to get more detailed infos from HANA_ML, like what is the unsupported SQL type it found?

Regards,

Mark

entry.title: Re: how to query and update standard Purchase Order from...

entry.title: Issue with BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE1 – Reservation Not Visible in MB23
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have created a reservation for a material using BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE1, and I received a success message. However, when I check in MB23, the reservation is not appearing.

Has anyone faced this issue before? Do I need to commit the transaction explicitly or check for any missing parameters?

Any guidance would be appreciated!

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud | Cost Estimation by Batch
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

My client (semiconductor manufacturing) is planning to use SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. There is a requirement where they want to analyse costs and manage price according to batches.

This case applies to Make to Stock (MTS) process.

For Example if I have 1 Semi-Finished Goods (SFG) A with two batches A.1, A.2. Each batch is manufactured with a different cost, due to batch A.2 consumed more raw materials/overhead costs than planned. 

Next these two batches A.1, A.2 are consumed in Production Orders to produce Finished-Goods (FG). Because these 2 SFG batches have different costs, the FG manufactured by these 2 batches have different costs as well.

And they want these costs to be reflected when they choose the FG to sell to customers - that is - when they choose the FG to sell, they can choose batches in this FG, with price of each batch referenced correctly from costs collected to manufacture them.

Currently I know if we use standard price for FG, the cost for all Production Orders will be collected in Pricediff, then distributed all to inventory/COGS based on quantity. All batches of 1 FG will have same price.

Is there a way to do this in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public? I tried to use this case Solved: Batch wise costing SAP public cloud - SAP Community but the links for costing don't work, and I know SAP has removed standard cost estimation by split valuation. Mixed costing does not work as well, since all batches they use are manufacturing batches.

Kindly enlighten me on this issue.

entry.title: Approval Process in Add to Learning History
entry.content:
Hi,

We would like to set up approval process when Users use the Add to Learning History and mark course completion. The approval is to be triggered to 2 different set of Approver group.

Example: There are Users of Location 'A' and Location 'B' with Approver 'A' and Approver 'B'

If Users of Location 'A' add the course to learning history, it should trigger approval to Approver 'A'. The Approver 'B' should not see the Users' 'A' approval requests. Likewise, if Users of Location 'B' add the course to learning history, it should trigger approval to Approver 'B'. The Approver 'A' should not see the Users' 'B' approval requests.

Any solution is appreciated. SAP SuccessFactors Learning 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Tenant migration made possible
entry.content:
Hi @Sycntegral ,

could you please clarify what you mean by:

  1. interdependencies between tables and APIs
  2. "how will this work?" If you have any specific question that is not covered in the blog, please specifiy it.
  3. The backup is the old tenant itself, as it holds all data needed. During the migration, we do not need a backup.

Thank you and BR,

Thore

entry.title: Prefilled Forms but different Matching ID
entry.content:
Hello,
 
We are acting in the B2B segment when it comes to sales and marketing processes. Videos/articles and white papers are made available via forms on our homepage.
 
To promote this gated content, we send emails to targeted contacts. The forms are pre-filled for more convenience and to provide a better customer journey. For this we use the “Outbound ID” function which is enabled on the url itself. 
 
Our current problem is that some customers/contacts forward the URL or email and other people (with other personal details) fill out the form. Due to the outbound ID, these two contacts will now be merged. Usually the orignal contacts gets a new name and email for their best record. 
 
I would now like to know how and whether there are other solutions, for example that the SMC on forms can be checked with the email, regarding match and merge logic. 
 
Does anyone here have ideas or feedback? How have you solved this problem? 
 
BR 
Sascha

entry.title: 在进行应收汇票的批量认领导入时，提示抵销科目的问题，提示未在公司代码中定义，请指教下，如何处理的

Note: Please note that this is flat load.

entry.title: Re: SAC for Planning - DATEDIFF with variable-member or i...
entry.content: thank you

entry.title: Re: Log for Financial Plan Data upload
entry.content:
Dear @Annika98, 

please note that the Import Financial Plan Data app serves only as an interface for uploading data into the system. It is designed specifically for administrators, and all data must be manually reviewed and maintained before uploading. While the tool includes technical checks, such as verifying the existence of master data in the target system, it does not incorporate business logic or audit functionalities.

If you require data audits, we recommend using the planning functionality available in SAP Analytics Cloud.
For details, see Auditing Activity.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: SAC for Planning - DATEDIFF with variable-member or i...
entry.content: I will try, thank you @MKreitlein 😄

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio process manager - attribute mass changes
entry.content:
Hello,

you can do that via the SAP Signavio API. More Info´s under: SAP Signavio Process Manager - REST API — API documentation 17.4 documentation

Greetings

entry.title: Re: Connection between SAP Datasphere and Azure Storage A...
entry.content:
Hi Joachim,

I've not got any real-world experience with Private Link but the documentation suggests it's not supported for SAP Datasphere yet - link attached.

regs

Matt

https://help.sap.com/docs/private-link/private-link1/what-is-sap-private-link-service?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Connection between SAP Datasphere and Azure Storage A...
entry.content:
Hi all,

I reached out to an SAP expert, and this is their answer:

It's not possible to use SAP Private link service to connect SAP Datasphere to Azure. Unless a BTP Service is explicitly listed in the documentation, along with guidelines how to create the connection, it's safe to determine that the scenario is not supported. Currently supported scenarios in SAP Private link service are connecting CAP applications (CF/Kyma) to Azure/AWS (according to the list of services described in the documentation). 

So, it's not possible.

Kind Regards,

Joachim

entry.title: Re: Connection between SAP Datasphere and Azure Storage A...
entry.content: Thanks Matt! I reached out to an SAP expert, and indeed it's not possible.

Best,
Alp

entry.title: SAP PS : Work Center and other fields not visible in Network (Internal Activity)
entry.content:
Hello All Experts,

I have two instances where Work Center is visible and another place where Work Center is not visible in Network (Internal Activity). 

Pic 1 - Work Center visible.

Pic 2 below where Work Center is NOT visible. 

How can I make Work Center visible ? What settings I am missing here ?

I have checked Network related field status already - its set to optional.  

Can expert guide on this ? 

Thank you. 

 

Regards,

Tanuj K.

entry.title: Segment E1EDP28 in outbound invoic
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I need to get the LIKP-TRAID field from delivery to invoice in the IDOC (INVOIC).

I found the TRAID field in the E1EDP28 segment, but when I create an IDOC from an invoice (IDOC_OUTPUT_INVOICE function), this segment is not populated.

Where can I populate the segment? (Is it SAP standard or do I need to use an exit?) And how?

Thanks in advance.

Regards

entry.title: Re: F-90 with posting key 70 to Asset AUC . ERROR "Acquis...
entry.content: While the error message is different the below note confirms the negative indicator setting in AUC. 0001555517 AA 617 for settlement of investment measures

entry.title: Dyanmic Breaks and Gaps in Working Time
entry.content:
Hi,

We are making use of dynamci breaks. The break gets deducted when an employee works more than 5.5 hours. 

Gaps in working time are counted towards break time. 

Currently if the employee takes a 15 minute before (5.5 hours into his shift) the dynamci break deducts the additional 15 minutes correctly. 

However, when an employee takes their 30 minute break after 5.5 hours into their shift, the shift automatically deducts the full 30 minutes (even though he has a gap of 15 minutes in his working time). This is not correct.

 

So if the 15 minute break is taken before 5.5 hours into the shift the system is behaving as expected and deducting a dynamic break of only 15 minutes, however, this is not the case if the 15 minute break is taken after the 5.5 hous. How do I prevent the full 30 minutes being deducted, if 15 minutes have been taken after 5.5 hours into the shift?breaks

Thanks,

Manon

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Value Map - SAP Signavio Renaming and Expansion
entry.content:
Dear community, 

We are excited to announce a significant update in our SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. The SAP Signavio Value Map has been renamed to Business Transformation Management. This change reflects our ongoing commitment to providing comprehensive support and guidance for organizations navigating their journey towards effective business transformation.

 

Spoiler
❕To access the direct link to the Business Transformation Management Value Map provided above, please ensure you have completed both "Get Started" steps the first time you log in.

What Value Maps Provide

SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps are structured, outcome-driven, guided paths that help you innovate and achieve your business goals with SAP solutions. They offer:

  • Expert Guidance: Access to SAP experts who provide you with the essential knowledge and best practices tailored to your specific needs.
  • Collaborative Forums: Opportunities to engage with other customers and SAP professionals to share experiences and insights.
  • Resource Collections: Curated collections of learning materials, tools, and methodologies that streamline your path to value realization.
  • Innovation Enablement: Support in leveraging the latest SAP technologies to drive innovation within your organization.

Expansion with SAP LeanIX Learning Assets

To further enrich the Business Transformation Management Value Map, we are thrilled to announce the integration of SAP LeanIX learning assets. SAP LeanIX is a leader in enterprise architecture and cloud governance solutions, and its inclusion offers enhanced resources to better manage IT landscapes and drive digital transformation.

With SAP LeanIX, you’ll gain access to cutting-edge learning tools and methodologies that support strategic IT planning and execution. This extension is designed to empower your teams with the skills and knowledge required to optimize processes and foster continuous improvement across your business operations.

We believe these enhancements will significantly contribute to your success in achieving your digital transformation goals. We look forward to supporting you on your journey through the newly enhanced Business Transformation Management Value Map.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...
entry.content:
In the context of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming (RAP) model, late determination refers to the execution of determinations during the save sequence, specifically at the end of a transaction when changes from the transactional buffer are  persisted to the database. This approach ensures that certain business logic or validations are applied just before the data
is saved, maintaining data integrity and consistency.

Key Aspects of Late Determination:

Timing: Late determinations are executed during the save sequence, after all modifications have been made but before the data is committed to the database.

Trigger Conditions: These determinations can be triggered by specific operations such as create, update, or delete, or by changes to particular fields.

Implementation: In the RAP handler method FOR DETERMINE within the local ABAP behavior pool, the business logic for the determination is implemented.

Benefits of Late Determination:

Data Consistency: Ensures that all necessary validations and business rules are applied just before data persistence, maintaining the integrity of the data.

Performance Optimization: By executing determinations at the end of the transaction, unnecessary computations during intermediate stages are avoided, potentially improving performance.

For more detailed information, you can refer to the following resources:

[RAP determination | ABAP Keyword Documentation] (https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/enUS/ABENBDL_DETERMINATIONS.html)

[ABAP RESTful Application Programming [24] – External Numbering and Managed Early Numbering – Discovering ABAP](https://discoveringabap.com/2023/03/02/abaprestfulapplicationprogramming24externalnumberingandmanagedearlynumbering/commentpage1/)

These resources provide comprehensive insights into the implementation and advantages of late determinations within the RAP model.

Note:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/determinations-in-abap-restful-programming-model/ba-p/13489868

https://discoveringabap.com/2023/03/02/abap-restful-application-programming-24-external-numbering-and-managed-early-numbering/

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABENBDL_DETERMINATIONS.html

entry.title: Re: How to handle errors for semantic key with number ran...
entry.content:
In the context of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming (RAP) model, late determination refers to the execution of determinations during the save sequence, specifically at the end of a transaction when changes from the transactional buffer are  persisted to the database. This approach ensures that certain business logic or validations are applied just before the data
is saved, maintaining data integrity and consistency.

Key Aspects of Late Determination:

Timing: Late determinations are executed during the save sequence, after all modifications have been made but before the data is committed to the database.

Trigger Conditions: These determinations can be triggered by specific operations such as create, update, or delete, or by changes to particular fields.

Implementation: In the RAP handler method FOR DETERMINE within the local ABAP behavior pool, the business logic for the determination is implemented.

Benefits of Late Determination:

Data Consistency: Ensures that all necessary validations and business rules are applied just before data persistence, maintaining the integrity of the data.

Performance Optimization: By executing determinations at the end of the transaction, unnecessary computations during intermediate stages are avoided, potentially improving performance.

For more detailed information, you can refer to the following resources:

[RAP determination | ABAP Keyword Documentation] (https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/enUS/ABENBDL_DETERMINATIONS.html)

[ABAP RESTful Application Programming [24] – External Numbering and Managed Early Numbering – Discovering ABAP](https://discoveringabap.com/2023/03/02/abaprestfulapplicationprogramming24externalnumberingandmanage...)

These resources provide comprehensive insights into the implementation and advantages of late determinations within the RAP model.

Note:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/determinations-in-abap-restful-programming-mode...

https://discoveringabap.com/2023/03/02/abap-restful-application-programming-24-external-numbering-an...

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABENBDL_DETERMINATIONS.html

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...
entry.content: Please avoid the above response

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
Hello @Vajrakanth_Siddu,

I am trying to create a table that contains the current date and the two years ago date to define a dynamic filter (Interval) for data filtering in the AM Model.

I have used the following features:
 - Data Builder
 - New SQL View

The Temporal view contanis two date, the current date and the two years ago date, as two different rows:

SELECT CURRENT_DATE AS date_value FROM "1TL_SD_DUMMYTABLE_001"
UNION ALL
SELECT (ADD_YEARS(CURRENT_DATE, -2)) AS date_value2 FROM "1TL_SD_DUMMYTABLE_001";

And I want to use it in a filter variable on the "Billing Date":

But in the preview of the AM, it looks like this:

So I'm trying to create a dynamic filter, which takes the two dates and use them as an interval.

Any help would be welcome.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...
entry.content:
Despite this limitation, there are several integration scenarios available between SAP Ariba and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. These include:

1. Supplier Management (SLP) – Bi-directional Sync: Ensures that supplier data is consistently updated between SAP Ariba and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

2. Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (4BL): Allows for the identification of supply sources for purchase requisitions using SAP Ariba Sourcing integrated with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

3. Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts (4AZ): Enables the integration of contract management processes between SAP Ariba and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

4. Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying (2NV): Provides a user-friendly procurement experience by integrating guided buying features of SAP Ariba with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

5. Source-to-Pay with SAP Business Network (42K): Facilitates end-to-end supplier collaboration using SAP Business Network integrated with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

These integrations are achieved using SOAP APIs through Managed Gateways Passthrough integration, eliminating the need for additional add-ons typically required for on-premise systems.

entry.title: Re: Setup and usage of contract accounts
entry.content:
Hello Sascha,

Thanks for your reply.
Makes sense to split it this way, also because we don't want to interfere with all IS-U related improvements and changes.

Best Regards,
Henk Hoogerbrug

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume from an OData V4 service using CAP...
entry.content:
First of all it is usually good to enable the following debug logger (also documented here: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/operating-applications/observability#predefined-loggers) to see the URLs which are being generated by CAP Java's Remote OData feature:  

 

logging.level:
  com.sap.cds.remote.odata: DEBUG

 

 This will likely show that the URL that is generated looks like this: https://services.odata.org/experimental/OData/OData.svc/NorthWind. This URL is not correct and is the root cause of the 404 error.

This URL is used because CAP Java always appends the service name to the end of the destination's URL. In your case however the service name "NorthWind" doesn't seem to be the OData service name used by the NorthWind API. It is "OData.svc" instead. Therefore you need to instruct CAP to use "OData.svc" as service name instead of the default service name "NorthWind" which is derived from the service name in the CDS model. Please adjust your remote service configuration like this:

 

spring:
  config.activate.on-profile: sandbox
cds:
  remote.services:
    '[NorthWind]':
      type: "odata-v4"
      http:
        service: "OData.svc"
      destination:
        properties:
          url: "https://services.odata.org/experimental/OData"

 

You can also find additional details how the URL for a remote service is constructed in our docs here: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/cqn-services/remote-services#configuring-the-url

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to display Custom Application Logs in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content: Hi Diego, thanks for replying so quickly. I also agree that this should be included by SAP. Regarding the first part you mentioned that you were able to do, if possible, could you elaborate a bit more? I’ve only recently started working with SAP Cloud, and any information I can get is really helpful.

Re: Unable to Post Payload to SOAP API using SAP CPI !

Glad it's now working as expected. Can you also mark this topic as answered and in case you have the time, you can also share your detailed actions for any additional people from the community that might face similar constraints in the future know how to solve it.

entry.content: All the technical scenarios for S/4 public cloud with Ariba solutions is covered. The question is about the Ariba multi-ERP (FPC) architecture for connection to S/4 public cloud.

Dear Norbert Kaps,
you can check usage of ABAP objects which are part of compatibility packages in our own coding with ATC variant "S/4HANA Readiness". The respective piece lists for compatibility package items are maintained as well in our Simplification item DB. 
Best regards,
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Data import of Local Currency only in the measure bas...
entry.content: This UI is not common. So it's new model on NEO tenant, right? In this case, can you just create additional empty column and map it to GC.

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content: In my desperation I have changed the ID column from type BIGINT to INTEGER - et voila - the error disappeared. So contrary to the documentation, BIGINT is not accepted as a datatype for the ID column. At least on my version of HANA_ML.

entry.title: Foreign currency amount in J_3RK_CORR_ITEMS Table
entry.content:
I am developing a Detailed Ledger report using two tables:

  • ACDOCA for open documents
  • J_3RK_CORR_ITEMS for cleared documents

The report includes two currency columns:

  • Local currency amount (HSL)
  • Foreign currency amount (KSL)

However, the J_3RK_CORR_ITEMS table does not have a foreign currency amount (KSL) field. I am considering joining it with ACDOCA to retrieve the foreign currency amount, but I want to confirm if this approach has any risks.

Could you please advise if this method is reliable? Are there any potential issues, such as data inconsistencies, that I should be aware of?

Thank you for your support!

entry.title: Can we include both Inbound and the Outbound Deliveries in a single Transportation Unit in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have a requirement from business wherein they want to include both the Inbound and the Outbound Deliveries in a single Transportation Unit in SAP EWM.

The expectation is that they would add all the outbound deliveries with different stop sequence in a TU.

Then they would also like to add Customer Return deliveries from customer or pick drop ship deliveries (Pick from vendor and directly drop to customer location) also to the same TU created in above step.

Hence the idea is to drop parcels to the customer location and also pick parcels from customer location as they are on the same route / location using their internal fleet of transport.

Could you please let me know if this is possible in the TU functionality.

If yes, how ?

If not, what is the alternative solution ?

Thanks & Regards

Vijay Imadi

entry.title: Re: How to add a new field to Business Volume Selection Criteria?
entry.content: Thanks for the very helpful step by step guide.

entry.title: Re: Canada Year End Reporting (T4/T4A)
entry.content: The Year End Reporting tool in SAP creates a fixed-length flat file for T4/T4A outsourcing, but you may need SAP documentation for the exact layout. Also, check if the Service Canada XML file (Mag Media) works for your printer. T4 vs. T4A depends on income type, and T4A-NR factors in residency.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA public cloud be one of the Multi-ERP (FPC) a...
entry.content:
Yes, integrating SAP Ariba in a Multi-ERP (Federated Process Control - FPC) architecture to support S/4HANA Public Cloud as one of the backends is technically feasible within a Parent-Child Ariba realm setup. In this configuration, a parent site groups all the child sites together, facilitating centralized management and integration across multiple ERP systems.

The SAP Ariba Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG) enables the integration of single or multiple SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA systems with Ariba Network and SAP Ariba solutions. This integration allows for efficient management of supplier interactions across the spend management lifecycle.

By leveraging the Parent-Child realm architecture in SAP Ariba, organizations can effectively manage and integrate multiple ERP systems, including S/4HANA Public Cloud, ensuring seamless procurement processes across diverse backend environments.

entry.title: Re: SHDB transaction is not checking S_BDC_MONI
entry.content: Did you check (RZ11) values of parameters such as bdc/bdel_auth_check or bdc/shdb/auth_check ?

entry.title: OP_API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002 unable to create HU items for existing HUs
entry.content:
During our implementation of OP_API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002 we have not found a way to create HU Items for existing Handling units via the delivery API. API Documentation only gives an example of simultaneous HU header & HU item creation via one batch request here, however our use case changing HU content after initial creation is not met.

Looking into using the Handling Unit API though it would be ideal to have this functionality included in the delivery API.

A post request on just HU items without HU header returns error "No header found for the HandlingUnitExternalId". 

 

 

{
   "error":{
      "code":"LOHUM_DEL_API/002",
      "message":{
         "lang":"en",
         "value":"No header found for the HandlingUnitExternalId 00000000000300000208"
      }
   }  
}

 

 

A batch request that includes PUT or PATCH on the HU header+ POST request(s) for new HU item returns error "HTTP method 'PUT' is not supported by this entityset (HU Header)". 

 

 

 

 

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: How to Create a Section for Work Center Views in the...

Hey
I'm David from the UI Theme Designer Team. It looks like your question has been tagged incorrectly. The UI Theme Designer is specifically for custom theming, and your question seems to go beyond its scope.

I’d recommend reaching out to other colleagues who might be better suited to help with this.
Let me know if you need any guidance on theming!

Cheers
David

many thanks,

Attila

entry.title: Re: How to handle errors for semantic key with number ran...

entry.title: Re: VK11 Vs VB21
entry.content:
the promotion & sales deal process and condition records have the same logic. 

But promotions & Sales deal is better for reporting and tracking.

entry.title: Re: FI - Period Open Schedule Autiomatically
entry.content:
You can schedule report RFOB5200 as job. See
How to Open and Close Posting Periods in Mass using RFOB5200

entry.title: SD Pricing
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

I have an issue regarding the calculation of the conditiion basis value for a manual discount ZD00 at item level for an item.

Here is the scenario:

A customer bought 105 pieces of a product. A manual discount of 6% is offered.

In my sales order,  in line item 10, i have maintained a manual condition (ZAPR) which is the price of the item. The manual price is 59,48 euro.

Total price of item: 59,48 * 105 =6.245,40 

Then i have added a manual discount (ZD00) of 6% which gives me 374,72 eur. This is calculated from the total price.

Then i have created a proforma invoice for line item 10. When i checked the condition price at item level, i see that the discount is doubled. 

It seems that the discount has been calculated by added the value 6.245,51 euro of an inactive condition type ZRFP which is a rebate factor with the value of the ZAPR price which is 6245,40 euro in the condition basis. Based on the condition basis, this discount has been calculated.

This discount is not correct and does not reflect the discount in the sales order.

Could you please tell me why the system has added the value of the rebate factor which that of the ZAPR price to calculated the discount in the proforma invoice?

@Lakshmipathi 

@rob_ariaans

entry.title: it is possible to export the queries created for Ticket searchThank you
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

it is possible to export the queries created for Ticket search in ITSM

Thank you

  • Read long text of error message OH 040 (and use search options of forum/support)

Thanks in advance!

Dear Team,

This is happening only for this invoice and this is new vendor .The SES AMOUNT Is showing correctly.

entry.title: Gantt Chart Drag and Drop feature
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

The sample examples on Gantt Chart provided by SAP showcase how to implement drag and drop of shapes along the horizontal axis, however it may be a challenge for some citizen developers to extend this feature to enable users to drag the shapes anywhere in the chart and not just horizontally.

This is a quick blog which will show you how to implement the same in a gantt chart with tree table.

  • Download the sample code provided on Basic Gantt Chart
  • Open the BasicGanttChart.view.xml file and update the event handler name as shown below.

  • Now implement the event handler in your controller code. The complete controller code is attached as a .txt file.

 

onShapeDrag: (oEvent) => {
          const oChartData = oController
            .getView()
            .byId("GanttChartContainer")
            .getModel("test")
            .getData();

          const sourcePath = oController
            .getView()
            .byId("ganttView")
            .getSelectedShapes()["order"][0]["objectInfoRef"]["contextObj"][
            "sPath"
          ];

          const targetPath =
            oEvent.getParameters()["targetData"]["objectInfo"]["contextObj"][
              "sPath"
            ];

          const oSourceShapeData = oController
            .getView()
            .byId("ganttView")
            .getSelectedShapes()["order"]["0"]["shapeData"];

          const oTargetShapeData =
            oEvent.getParameters()["targetData"]["shapeTimestamp"];

          oController._travelTreeAndDelete(
            oChartData,
            sourcePath,
            oSourceShapeData
          );
          oController._travelTreeAndUpdate(
            oChartData,
            targetPath,
            oTargetShapeData
          );

          oController
            .getView()
            .byId("GanttChartContainer")
            .getModel("test")
            .setData(oChartData);
        },
        _travelTreeAndDelete: (oData, sPath, oShapeData) => {
          const oArr = sPath.split("/");
          for (let idx = 1; idx < oArr.length; idx++) {
            oData = oData[`${oArr[idx]}`];
          }

          for (let [idx, item] of oData["order"].entries()) {
            if (
              item["startTime"] === oShapeData["startTime"] &&
              item["endTime"] === oShapeData["endTime"]
            ) {
              oController._deletedLevel = item["level"];
              oData["order"].splice(idx, 1);
            }
          }
        },
        _travelTreeAndUpdate: (oData, sPath, oShapeData) => {
          const oArr = sPath.split("/");
          for (let idx = 1; idx < oArr.length; idx++) {
            oData = oData[`${oArr[idx]}`];
          }

          oData["order"].push({
            startTime: sap.gantt.misc.Format.dateToAbapTimestamp(
              new Date(oShapeData["startTime"])
            ),
            endTime: sap.gantt.misc.Format.dateToAbapTimestamp(
              new Date(oShapeData["endTime"])
            ),
            level: oController._deletedLevel,
          });
        },

 

Now run your application. You will be able to drag and drop shapes anywhere in the chart and not restricted to the horizontal axis only.

Thank you for visiting this blog. If you have any requests on enhancing this further, feel free to drop a comment below.

entry.title: Re: F-90 with posting key 70 to Asset AUC . ERROR "Acquis...
entry.content:
Do you have any other derived areas based on area 01? Even if 01 is positive you can get this error if one of the derived areas dependent of area 01 will become negative. It can be a derived area of 01 or also areas within same accounting principle as area 01.

Example

Area 03 = Area 02 - Area 01 

In example above, if you are posting additional values in Area 01, Area 03 can become negative, leading to this message.

entry.title: Re: Unable to close project / WBS
entry.content:
If no commitment is displayed with CJI5, CN41N or ME23n (after Reducing Purchase Order Commitments to Zero)

There may be a discrepancy between the detail tables and the summary tables (e.g. RPSCO), some reports such as PS_RPSCO_REBUILD (CJEN) can rebuild the table.

entry.title: Re: Gantt Chart in SAP UI5 - Drag and Drop functionality between 2 Charts
entry.content: @former_member344161, @danielspiess : Tree Table Gantt Chart with drag and drop

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Ariba Central Procurement – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
This blog post contains the latest capabilities and features in SAP Ariba Central Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Click here for a recap of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 release highlights.

Central Purchasing

With this release,

  • You can now use the configured custom fields in the Mass Changes to Centrally Managed Purchase Requisitions in Offline Mode.
  • For a centrally managed purchase order workflow, you can view the actual approver name of all the roles (such as Manager of last Approver, Agent Determination by BAdI, Self-service Approver, Central Purchasing and Workflow Administration) of the next planned step.
  • When you add, modify, or delete header or item texts, you can view the configured texts in the centrally managed purchase order output in the hub system.
  • You can now create a central supplier confirmation for a centrally managed purchase order with E (Enhanced Limit) in the Manage Central Supplier Confirmations app. You can add, view and change confirmation items of item categories Standard (blank), lean services and E (Enhanced Limit).
  • You can now view the Approval Details facet for a central supplier confirmation in the Manage Central Supplier Confirmations app.

Central Purchase Contracts

With this release,

  • You can now undelete the central purchase contract line items and header and line distribution lines by using the new button Restore. The Restore feature is available for central purchase contracts in the apps Manage Central Purchase Contracts and Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts for central purchase contracts in offline and online mode.
  • You can view the new field Planned Delivery Duration in Days, which specifies the delivery timeframe in days, under GR / IR Control facet within the item section of the app Manage Central Purchase Contracts for central purchase contracts and central purchase contract hierarchies and the app Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts in online and offline modes.
  • You can view/use additional fields that are added to the app Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts for central purchase contracts and central purchase contract hierarchies.

 

Section

Fields Added

Description

Central Purchase Contract Header

Hierarchy Central Cont. Header

PurgContractIsInPreparation

Purchase Contract is in-Preparation

Central Purchase Contract Item

Hierarchy Central Cont. Item

OrderPriceUnitToOrderUnitNmrtr

OrdPriceUnitToOrderUnitDnmntr

PlannedDeliveryDurationInDays

PurchasingDocumentItemCategory

Numerator for Conversion of Order Price Unit into Order Unit

Denominator for Conversion of Order Price Unit into Order Unit

Planned Delivery Time in Days

Item Category in Purchasing Document

Central Purchase Contract Header Distribution

Hierarchy Cont. Header Dist.

IncotermsClassification

IncotermsLocation1

Incoterms (Part 1)

Incoterms Location 1

Central Purchase Contract Item Distribution

Hierarchy Cont. Item Dist.

IncotermsClassification

IncotermsLocation1

Incoterms (Part 1)

Incoterms Location 1

Central Purchase Contract Item Pricing

Hierarchy Cont. Item Pricing

ConditionToBaseQtyDnmntr

ConditionToBaseQtyNmrtr

Denominator for Converting Condition Units to Base Units

Numerator for Converting Condition Units to Base Units

 

  • You can use the Save in Preparation field in the Central Contract Header and Hierarchy Central Cont. Header worksheets to save new central purchase contracts and central purchase contract hierarchies respectively without releasing them.
  • You can now upload or download the Scale values for item condition types that are configured in the new worksheet Central Contract Item Price Scale during the creation of central purchase contracts or in the new worksheet Hierarchy Cont. Item Prc Scale of central purchase contract hierarchies using the Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts app in the offline mode. You can edit the values in the Scale field for the relevant condition types even if there are existing values or newly created, and use the scale types A (Base scale or From scale) and B ( To - scale ). It is not applicable to scale type D (Graduated-to interval scale).
  • You can maintain separate conversion factors for the quantity based on main condition and supplementary conditions in the unit of measure, for item conditions of a central purchase contract and central purchase contract hierarchy in the Manage Central Purchase Contracts app.
  • You can now select multiple distribution lines to perform any action such as Create or Delete or Restore, and Block or Unblock in the Manage Central Purchase Contracts app.

Commodities in Central Purchase Contracts

With this release, the Commodities feature of the apps Manage Central Purchase Contracts for central purchase contracts and central purchase contract hierarchies and Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts in both online and offline modes is removed and is no longer available.

Furthermore, the entity Commodity Quantity (A_CntrlPurContrItmCmmdtyQty) of the OData V2 API Central Purchase Contract (API_CENTRAL_PURCHASECONTRACT) is also removed and is no longer available with this release.

Central Purchasing Analytics

With this release, as a central purchaser, you can monitor the open central purchase requisition items (along with open quantities) using the app Monitor Central Purchase Requisition Items. Deleted and closed central purchase requisition items are not visible in this app.

Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement (6BJ)

With this scope item, you can now connect your hub system (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) to SAP Business Network.

Order confirmations can be created by suppliers in the SAP Business Network for centrally managed purchase orders. These order confirmations are transferred back to the hub system and further to the connected system.

 

Configuration in Central Procurement

With this release,

  • You can now use a new field Product Type Group (PRODUCTTYPE) for the customizing activities Create Condition Tables and Change Condition Tables.
  • The SSCUI is enabled for the customizing activity Define Number Ranges for Version Management so you can configure version number range for purchase contract and central purchase contract.
  • The BAdI Change of Central Purchase Contract Item Before Saving, has been enhanced with the new field procmthubrefcdconnectedsystem. You can now configure the default connected system, which can be viewed in the Connected System ID field for material field of a central purchase contract item in the Manage Central Purchase Contracts app. In the material value help dialog, the configured connected system ID value will be defaulted to the filter input and if required this can be changed according to the requirement.

This is a brief overview of the new features available in SAP Ariba Central Procurement for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. I hope you found this blog post insightful.

Click here for the complete release highlights of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.

Share your feedback and thoughts in the comments section, and in case you have further questions, please post them on the community page using the link below:

Ask a question

For more information on SAP Ariba Central Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, check out the link: SAP Help Portal Product Page

entry.title: What’s New in Purchase Requisitions - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Hi Friends, let us look at some of the exciting features in purchase requisitions for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. 

Order Acknowledgment and Advance Shipping Notification for a Purchase Requisition Item

Using the app My Purchase Requisitions - New, in the context of central requisitioning, you can now view the follow-on documents for a purchase requisition item such as Order Acknowledgment, which is a confirmation from a supplier to deliver specified quantities of products at specified prices within specified times, and Advance Shipping Notification, which contains information provided by the supplier about times of issue and receipt for specific quantities of ordered materials.

As an employee, in the My Purchase Requisitions – New app, you can now view the Order Acknowledgment and Advance Shipping Notification in the Status Details of the purchase requisition item.

For service items or enhanced limit, the order acknowledgment and advance shipping notification document is not supported and will not appear in the Status Details.

View Parked Invoice in the Status Details of a Purchase Requisition Item

Using the app, My Purchase Requisitions – New app, you can now view the parked invoice as a follow-on document for the purchase requisition item.

It can be viewed in the Status Details of the purchase requisition item. The parked invoice is a type of supplier invoice, where the already entered data is retained until the required information to post the invoice document is obtained.

Support of Advanced Purchase Order Scenarios in Process Purchase Requisitions (V2)

You can now create purchase orders for purchase requisitions with advanced fields from the Process Purchase Requisition (V2) app.

For instance, when you are creating a purchase order for a purchase requisition with purchase requisition item category U (stock transfer PR) or purchase requisition document type UB, a message is displayed with simulation check performed for Manage Purchase Orders, then the purchase order is automatically created and you are navigated to the Display Purchase Order - Advanced app to view the purchase order.

Process Purchase Requisitions

This app is deprecated and will be removed from the SAP Fiori Launchpad in a future release. We recommend that you switch to the successor app Process Purchase Requisitions (V2) at your earliest convenience.

CDS Views in Purchase Requisition

For the CDS view, I_PurchaseRequisitionItemAPI01, the following fields are now available

  • I_Purchaserequisitionitem.IsPurReqnCmplt
  • I_Purchaserequisitionitem.PurReqnItemBlockingReasonText
  • I_Purchaserequisitionitem.InventorySpecialStockType
  • I_Purchaserequisitionitem.QuotaArrangement
  • I_Purchaserequisitionitem.QuotaArrangementItem

OData V2 API: Purchase Requisition

The API service API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV is deprecated with the release SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402. We recommend you switch to the successor API service API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2 as soon as possible.

Identity and Access Management

The following changes to identity and access management (IAM) objects have been delivered for Purchase Requisitions.

Name of IAM Object

Object Type

Changes

Details

Relevant Scope Items

Materials Management - Purchase Requisition Completion (SAP_MM_BC_PR_COMPLETION_PC)

Materials Management - Advanced Purchase Requisitions (SAP_MM_BC_PR_MANAGE_PC)

Materials Management - Purchasing Document Display(SAP_MM_BC_PURCH_DOC_DSP_PC)

Materials Management - Employee Self Services V2 (SAP_MM_BC_SSPPR_MANAGE_PC)

Business Catalog

Removal of Phase In

The phase in is removed and restriction is now enforced for the authorization object PROJ_BILLG.

18J (Requisitioning), O8J (Requisitioning), BMD (Purchase Contract), BNX (Consumable Purchasing), 1XI (Central Requisitioning)

Materials Management - Purchase Requisitions (SAP_MM_BC_PR_PROCESS_MC)

Business Catalog

Enable restriction for F4 and Read

The restriction types AUART (Sales Document Type), AUFART_WERKS (Order Type/Plant), BEGRU_MAT (Authorization Group for Product) and BEGRU_MATKL (Authorization Group for Product Group) are updated.

18J (Requisitioning)

Materials Management - Purchase Requisitions (SAP_MM_BC_PR_PROCESS_MC)

Business Catalog

Enable restriction for F4

The restriction types AUTYP (Order Category), BEGRU_MTART (Authorization Group for Product Type), and S4PPM_PR1 (Enterprise Project) are updated.

18J (Requisitioning)

Materials Management - Purchase Requisitions (SAP_MM_BC_PR_PROCESS_MC)

Business Catalog

Enable restriction for Read

The restriction types PP_MRP_CTRLR (MRP Controller), BEGRU_LFA1 (Authorization Group for Suppliers), KTOPL (Chart of Accounts) and KTOKK (Supplier Account Group), are updated.

18J (Requisitioning)

 

Guided Buying for Central Procurement with SAP Ariba Buying (3EN)

With this feature, the street fields, Street 2 and Street 3 from the 3EN (Guided Buying for Central Procurement with SAP Ariba Buying) purchase requisition are replicated and are available in the central system purchase requisition. You can view them using the Create Purchase Requisition - Advanced app.

Well, that is all that we have for 2502! If you have any questions or thoughts, feel free to post in the comments section.


For more information on Purchase Requisitions - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition check out: All About Purchase Requisitions - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content: That's great, happy that it's working now. Can you please check whether BIGINT works in the latest hana_ml version. (currently 2.23.25021400). Our Product Group can then check whether this is an issue with the algorithm or the documentation.

entry.title: Re: Setting up BTP ABAP Environment transport management with Cloud Transport Management Service
entry.content:
Hi Aoyang,

We have a BTP-ABAP Dev, QA and PROD systems. I want to make sure Developers will have Display only access for BTP-ABAP in QA and PROD. ie, lock developers from creating Changes(transports). How do I achieve this.

Regards

Kasi

entry.title: WESLOG XML not picking Material
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are facing one issue ,

We have added DMARC-DISMM IN BD52 in message type WES_MERCHANDISE, But when we are changing MRP TYPE in MM42 logistics store tab  ,this article/ material is picking or not generating to XML in WESLOG. Please note this is generating entries in BDCP2 table.

What we can do can anyone suggest ? can we use any other message type like WP_PLU or any other solution from functional or ABAP side?

 

Note  when we are changing SHORT TEXT IN MM42 weslog picking material in XML.

Please share some solution.

Thanks

Gourav.

entry.title: Re: RAISE SHORTDUMP- Handling a program-driven runtime error
entry.content:
Hello Safah,

thank you very much for the post.

I have a question: What if the "RAISE EXCEPTION" does not use a class, so:

FUNCTION abc
  EXCEPTIONS
    UNKNOWN_ERROR.
    
  RAISE UNKNOWN_ERROR.
 
ENDFUNCTION.

CLASS ABC.
   METHOD a.
     CALL FUNCTION 'abc'.
   ENDMETHOD.

   METHOD b.
      a()  <-- HOW TO CATCH "UNKNOWN_ERROR" HERE WITHOUT MODIFYING METHOD a?
   ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Platform ABAP Environment - Lifecycle Management - Introduction
entry.content:
Hi Andre,

We have Dev, QA and PROD systems on BTP-ABAP Environment . I want to make sure Developers will have Display only access in QA and PROD. ie, lock developers from creating Changes(transports). How do I achieve this. is there a specific display only role for BTP-ABAP Environment.  Developer want to create Application jobs using ABAP-FLP but itseems not possible without providing access to BTP-ABAP Environment in QA and PROD.

Regards

Kasi

entry.title: Re: Mass changes to purchasing info record
entry.content: I would translate Meldung as Notification in this case xxx

entry.title: Show UDF from SAP B1 in SAP Customer Checkout Sales screen
entry.content:
Hello dear community members,

I am new to SAP Customer Checkout. I have created UDF in items master data of SAP B1. Plus, I have integrated SAP Customer Checkout with SAP Business One. My question is how can I show this UDF value in the items' search screen and sales screen in SAP Customer Checkout? I have attached their photos.

entry.title: Query for Creation of Destination DEV, TEST, PREPROD and PROD with OAUTHClientCredentials
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I have one scenario/requirement to create Destinations for CTMS configuration.
The Destinations TransportManagementService, ContentAssemblyService and CloudIntegration were already created with the help of Service Key from Instances of TransportManagementService, ContentAgentService with Standard Plan and PI with API Plan. The relevant details (URL, Client ID, Client Secret and Token URL) were retrieved from their respective service keys.

We also needed to create the Destinations of Deploy-Service like DEV, TEST, Pre-Prod, and Prod. They are currently configured with the Basic Authentication.

Now the requirement is to change the Authentication type from Basic to OAUTH2ClientCredentials for these destinations.
My Question is if we change the authentication from BASIC to OAUTH2ClientCredentials for these destinations to have more secure authentication, then from which Instances we can retreive the Client URL, Client ID, Client Secret and Token URL Information? 
All the Destinations are created under DEV Subaccount as of now.
And is there any need of subscribing/instance creation in other subaccounts also? 

Any Guidance and Helps are appreciated.

======================
Thanks and Regards
Anurag
======================
